solute.description: This molecule consists of a six-membered aromatic benzene ring directly bonded to a carboxylic acid functional group. As the simplest aromatic carboxylic acid, its structure features a single carboxyl substituent (–COOH) replacing one hydrogen atom on the phenyl ring. The molecule is small and planar, characterized by a non-polar hydrophobic core and a highly polar, acidic head group. It possesses one hydrogen bond donor (the hydroxyl group) and two hydrogen bond acceptors (the carbonyl and hydroxyl oxygens).

solvent.description: This molecule is a straight-chain, unbranched alkane consisting of eight carbon atoms linked by single bonds. It lacks any functional groups or heteroatoms, being composed entirely of saturated carbon and hydrogen atoms. The structure is highly nonpolar and lacks both hydrogen bond donors and acceptors. Its distinctive character arises from its linear aliphatic backbone and uniform electron density across the medium-sized hydrocarbon chain.
